DSDS 0.1
Overview Root Schema
example extensions link metadata presentation rich-text status usecase
component pattern style theme token
accessibility anatomy api best-practice content design-specifications guideline interaction motion principle purpose scale state variant

DSDS Scale Guideline

Documents an ordered sequence of values that forms a visual scale — a type scale, spacing scale, elevation scale, or any other progression of design token values. Each step in the scale references a design token and optionally provides a human-readable label, a resolved value for display, and usage notes. Scales establish the constrained set of values that designers and engineers choose from, replacing ad hoc values with a deliberate, harmonious progression.

Source: guidelines/scale.schema.json

2 definitions in this file:

  • scaleStep
  • scale

scaleStep

A single step in an ordered scale. Each step references a design token that provides the concrete value for that position in the progression. The optional label, value, and description provide context for documentation display without duplicating the token's authoritative definition.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
tokenstringrequiredThe token name for this step. MUST reference a token defined in the token schema (e.g., 'space-4', 'font-size-300', 'elevation-low'). This is the single source of truth for the step's value — the optional `value` property is a display convenience only.
labelstringoptionalA human-readable label for this step in the scale (e.g., 'sm', 'md', 'lg', '2xl', 'body', 'heading-1'). Labels provide a shorthand vocabulary that designers and engineers use to communicate about scale positions without referring to raw values.
valuestringoptionalThe resolved value for display purposes (e.g., '16px', '1rem', '#1a1a1a', '400'). This is a documentation convenience — the authoritative value lives in the referenced token. Tools MAY populate this automatically from the token catalog.
descriptionrichTextoptionalUsage notes for this specific step — when to use it, what it pairs with, and any constraints (e.g., 'The base unit. Use for default internal component padding.', 'Reserved for page-level hero sections only.').

References: richText

scale

Documents an ordered scale — a deliberate progression of values that constrains design decisions to a harmonious, consistent set. Scales are the primary mechanism for spacing systems, type hierarchies, elevation levels, and other graduated visual attributes. The steps array is ordered from smallest/lowest to largest/highest, and tools SHOULD preserve this ordering for display.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
kind"scale"requiredIdentifies this guideline as a scale specification.
namestringrequiredMachine-readable name of the scale (e.g., 'spacing-scale', 'type-scale', 'elevation-scale'). Used for cross-referencing and tooling.
descriptionrichTextrequiredWhat this scale is, how it was constructed (e.g., geometric progression, modular scale), and the rules for using it. This is the place to document the scale's rationale and constraints — why these specific values were chosen and when it's appropriate to deviate.
stepsscaleStep[]requiredThe ordered steps in the scale, from smallest/lowest to largest/highest. The ordering is significant — tools SHOULD preserve it for display, and consumers SHOULD treat adjacent steps as the natural next-smaller and next-larger options when adjusting a value.
Min items: 1
displayNamestringoptionalHuman-readable name of the scale (e.g., 'Spacing Scale', 'Type Scale', 'Elevation Levels').

References: richText, scaleStep
